isolation.guidanceSection: Flow Validator Guidance: Mini shell source/build surface
isolation.inspectionMode: Read-only source and generated-artifact inspection plus copying shared evidence into the assigned evidence directory.
constraints: Did not rerun write-heavy mini build or validate scripts.
No WeChat runtime, device, upload, or live backend claims made.

commandsRun: Read/Grep inspection of mission guidance, validation contract, mini source files, repo artifacts, and shared validator evidence.
python3 copy of shared local-preview.html, validation-summary.json, and npm-test-build-validate.txt into the assigned mission evidence directory.

assertions.title: The checked-in Mini project preserves import-shell source configuration with AppID wiring and non-local runtime targets
assertions.proofSummary: The checked-in mini project keeps AppID wx1d02a932c4f9a4ae, compileType=miniprogram, and a non-loopback placeholder-preview backend target. The shared validator log confirms the runtime boundary passed source/build validation without claiming real runtime success.

sourceReferences.note: Preserves the shared AppID, miniprogram compileType, and publish-facing project name TradingAgentsMiniImportShell.
Defines the checked-in AppID plus placeholder-preview mode and non-loopback baseUrl https://mini-runtime-placeholder.invalid.
Registers the real Home, Watch, and Account shell surfaces under the top-level mini app.

artifactReferences.note: Copied summary artifact shows placeholder-preview runtime boundary and the three primary shell surfaces.
Shared validator output confirms the checked-in runtime config preserves the AppID/non-loopback target and that mini_validate passed.

assertions.title: The checked-in Mini shell keeps a configurable placeholder-to-runtime backend boundary
assertions.proofSummary: The shipped shell defaults to placeholder-preview mode, loads operator-private overrides from mini/config/runtime.local.js or mini/project.private.config.json, and wires apiBaseUrl from getRuntimeConfig() so later HTTPS runtime swaps happen through configuration rather than page or business-logic edits.

sourceReferences.note: Defines the default placeholder-preview runtime plus local/private override paths and the no-source-edit swap summary.
Keeps override paths under versioned config, validates loopback targets, and exposes checked-in versus operator-overridden runtime getters.
Initializes globalData.apiBaseUrl, runtimeMode, and validation metadata from getRuntimeConfig().

artifactReferences.note: Copied preview artifact calls out the shared config, local override, and private DevTools config paths.
Copied summary artifact records placeholder-preview mode, non-loopback baseUrl, and the local/private override paths.
Shared validator output confirms operator overrides can swap runtime targets without changing shared shell metadata and that the runtime boundary proof passed.

assertions.title: The local Mini surface stays explicit about non-runtime validation
assertions.proofSummary: The checked-in preview and generated summary explicitly label the evidence as source/build import-shell validation with a placeholder-safe runtime default, and they disclaim any real WeChat simulator, device, upload, or live backend success.

sourceReferences.note: Ships runtimeDisclosure, previewDisclosure, and previewEvidenceLabel copy that keeps the local Mini proof honest.

artifactReferences.note: Copied preview artifact labels the shell as local-only source/build proof and explicitly says it is not evidence of real WeChat runtime success.
Copied summary artifact records validationMode=source-build-import-shell plus matching runtime disclaimers.
Shared validator/build log repeats the source/build-only validation posture and the absence of runtime/upload/live-backend claims.

assertions.title: The Mini protected digest surface renders one authenticated payload-backed card per canonical stock_code
assertions.proofSummary: The Watch surface authenticates through the shared bearer-session boundary, dedupes payload rows down to one rendered card per canonical stock_code, maps only compact digest fields for rendering, and preserves waiting/placeholder cards when no ready digest exists.

sourceReferences.note: Uses POST /api/auth/login and GET /api/watch/digests with the bearer session the Mini surface persists.
selectCompactDigestCards dedupes by stock_code and mapDigestCard projects only compact shared fields into the rendered Watch model.
Watch distinguishes waiting versus ready, reports rendered stock_code values, and states that it consumes only shared compact fields.
Preview data defines the compact field contract, includes duplicate 600519 rows, keeps a waiting 000001 card, and includes extra browser/report fields that the boundary must ignore.

artifactReferences.note: Copied preview artifact shows waiting and ready Watch states, one-card-per-stock_code proof, and compact shared field messaging.
Copied summary artifact records 3 raw payload rows rendered as 2 cards with 1 placeholder retained and lists the compact field keys.
Shared test/build log confirms bearer token reuse on the protected read path and the one-card-per-stock_code/non-compact-field stripping behavior.

assertions.title: Auth failures do not fall through to stale, mock, or empty-success watch cards
assertions.proofSummary: Missing or invalid bearer sessions return authRequired with empty cards, controller auth-required transitions clear any rendered protected content, and the Watch copy explicitly says it will not show mock, stale, or fake-success empty cards.

sourceReferences.note: No session or HTTP 401 produces authRequired=true with cards=[] and clears the persisted session.
applyAuthRequiredState clears cards, renderedStockCodes, and counts whenever auth is missing or digest auth fails.
Watch source says no stale cards are reused during loading and no mock/stale/fake-success cards appear in auth-required state.

artifactReferences.note: Copied preview artifact shows distinct auth-required and authenticated-empty states instead of a fake successful empty watch surface.
Copied summary artifact records the authRequired code and keeps authenticated-empty separate from auth-required.
Shared test/build log confirms auth failures clear protected cards and that Watch keeps loading/auth-required/authenticated-empty/waiting/ready states distinct.

assertions.title: The Mini boundary stays contract-first and presentation-neutral
assertions.proofSummary: The Mini boundary remains thin: it defines and consumes only the compact shared digest-card fields needed for mobile rendering, strips preview-only browser/report extras, and renders from mapped mobile-safe data without route-context or real-runtime dependencies.

sourceReferences.note: Defines compactFieldKeys and includes non-contract extras such as symbol, route_name, report_body, report_html, and browser_route_context in preview payloads.
Copies only compact contract fields into the normalized card model the Mini surface renders.
Watch surfaces stock identity, quote, summary, and status fields from the mapped compact card model only.

artifactReferences.note: Copied preview artifact calls out one-card-per-stock_code behavior and compact shared field consumption.
Copied summary artifact records renderedStockCodes and the compact field keys used by the Mini boundary.
Shared validator output confirms the stock_code dedupe and non-compact field stripping behavior.

summary: Tested 6 assigned source/build runtime-boundary assertions: 6 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Evidence stays limited to checked-in mini source/build proof and explicitly defers real WeChat runtime, device, upload, and live backend validation.
